creator: c.kworr@gmail.com
text:
27.06.2013 18:03, Chris Dukes wrote:
> On Thu, Jun 27, 2013 at 04:54:10PM +0300, Volodymyr Kostyrko wrote:
>> Hello.
>>
>> Builds fine for me on stable/9 with CLANG_AS_CC. Maybe this one can
>> be closed?
>
> Yep, it still fails on build with LUA enabled and clang as the compiler.

Missed the LUA part at first sight. Works fine with LUA enabled.

>
> gmake[4]: Entering directory `/wrkdirs/usr/ports/graphics/graphviz/work/graphviz-2.30.1/tclpkg/gv'
>    CXX    libgv_lua_la-gv.lo
> error: invalid argument '-std=gnu89' not allowed with 'C++/ObjC++'

Here is your poison. My build doesn't set -std=gnu89. I have no such 
setting both on release/9.1 and stable/9 machines. And both machines can 
build graphviz with LUA fine.

Can you please check that:

  * port Makefile;
  * ports and system Mk scripts;
  * /etc/src.conf;
  * /etc/make.conf;

doesn't contain any redeclarations of USE_CSTD?

> gmake[4]: *** [libgv_lua_la-gv.lo] Error 1
>
>
> Clang is being enabled with
> CC=clang
> CXX=clang++
> CPP=clang-cpp
>
> I'm not seeing CLANG_AS_CC in /usr/share/mk/* nor $PORTSDIR/Mk/*
> Jails for builds are FreeBSD 9.1 plus patches as provided by freebsd-update
> and do not seem to have this feature yet.

WITH_CLANG_IS_CC is a buildworld knob making # cc -v
FreeBSD clang version 3.2 (tags/RELEASE_32/final 170710) 20121221
Target: x86_64-unknown-freebsd9.1
Thread model: posix

For me this is a preferred way of using clang, all my servers are built 
WITH_CLANG_IS_CC.

-- 
Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.
